root_text.an6.16:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā bhaggesu viharati susumāragire bhesakaḷāvane migadāye.
root_text.an6.16:1.2: Tena kho pana samayena nakulapitā gahapati ābādhiko hoti dukkhito bāḷhagilāno.
root_text.an6.16:1.3: Atha kho nakulamātā gahapatānī nakulapitaraṁ gahapatiṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an6.16:2.4: Siyā kho pana te, gahapati, evamassa:
root_text.an6.16:2.5: ‘na nakulamātā gahapatānī mamaccayena sakkhissati dārake posetuṁ, gharāvāsaṁ santharitun’ti.
root_text.an6.16:2.6: Na kho panetaṁ, gahapati, evaṁ daṭṭhabbaṁ.
root_text.an6.16:2.7: Kusalāhaṁ, gahapati, kappāsaṁ kantituṁ veṇiṁ olikhituṁ.
root_text.an6.16:2.8: Sakkomahaṁ, gahapati, tavaccayena dārake posetuṁ, gharāvāsaṁ santharituṁ.
root_text.an6.16:2.9: Tasmātiha tvaṁ, gahapati, mā sāpekkho kālamakāsi.
root_text.an6.16:3.1: Siyā kho pana te, gahapati, evamassa:
root_text.an6.16:3.3: Na kho panetaṁ, gahapati, evaṁ daṭṭhabbaṁ.
root_text.an6.16:3.4: Tvañceva kho, gahapati, jānāsi ahañca, yaṁ no soḷasavassāni gahaṭṭhakaṁ brahmacariyaṁ samāciṇṇaṁ.
root_text.an6.16:3.5: Tasmātiha tvaṁ, gahapati, mā sāpekkho kālamakāsi.
root_text.an6.16:4.1: Siyā kho pana te, gahapati, evamassa:
root_text.an6.16:4.2: ‘nakulamātā gahapatānī mamaccayena na dassanakāmā bhavissati bhagavato na dassanakāmā bhikkhusaṅghassā’ti.
root_text.an6.16:4.3: Na kho panetaṁ, gahapati, evaṁ daṭṭhabbaṁ.
root_text.an6.16:4.4: Ahañhi, gahapati, tavaccayena dassanakāmatarā ceva bhavissāmi bhagavato, dassanakāmatarā ca bhikkhusaṅghassa.
root_text.an6.16:4.5: Tasmātiha tvaṁ, gahapati, mā sāpekkho kālamakāsi.
root_text.an6.16:5.1: Siyā kho pana te, gahapati, evamassa:
root_text.an6.16:5.3: Na kho panetaṁ, gahapati, evaṁ daṭṭhabbaṁ.
root_text.an6.16:5.4: Yāvatā kho, gahapati, tassa bhagavato sāvikā gihī odātavasanā sīlesu paripūrakāriniyo, ahaṁ tāsaṁ aññatarā.
root_text.an6.16:5.5: Yassa kho panassa kaṅkhā vā vimati vā—ayaṁ so bhagavā arahaṁ sammāsambuddho bhaggesu viharati susumāragire bhesakaḷāvane migadāye—taṁ bhagavantaṁ upasaṅkamitvā pucchatu.
root_text.an6.16:5.6: Tasmātiha tvaṁ, gahapati, mā sāpekkho kālamakāsi.
root_text.an6.16:6.1: Siyā kho pana te, gahapati, evamassa:
root_text.an6.16:6.3: Na kho panetaṁ, gahapati, evaṁ daṭṭhabbaṁ.
root_text.an6.16:6.4: Yāvatā kho, gahapati, tassa bhagavato sāvikā gihī odātavasanā lābhiniyo ajjhattaṁ cetosamathassa, ahaṁ tāsaṁ aññatarā.
root_text.an6.16:6.5: Yassa kho panassa kaṅkhā vā vimati vā—ayaṁ so bhagavā arahaṁ sammāsambuddho bhaggesu viharati susumāragire bhesakaḷāvane migadāye—taṁ bhagavantaṁ upasaṅkamitvā pucchatu.
root_text.an6.16:6.6: Tasmātiha tvaṁ, gahapati, mā sāpekkho kālamakāsi.
root_text.an6.16:7.1: Siyā kho pana te, gahapati, evamassa:
root_text.an6.16:7.2: ‘na nakulamātā gahapatānī imasmiṁ dhammavinaye ogādhappattā patigādhappattā assāsappattā tiṇṇavicikicchā vigatakathaṅkathā vesārajjappattā aparappaccayā satthusāsane viharatī’ti.
root_text.an6.16:7.3: Na kho panetaṁ, gahapati, evaṁ daṭṭhabbaṁ.
root_text.an6.16:7.4: Yāvatā kho, gahapati, tassa bhagavato sāvikā gihī odātavasanā imasmiṁ dhammavinaye ogādhappattā patigādhappattā assāsappattā tiṇṇavicikicchā vigatakathaṅkathā vesārajjappattā aparappaccayā satthusāsane viharanti, ahaṁ tāsaṁ aññatarā.
root_text.an6.16:7.5: Yassa kho panassa kaṅkhā vā vimati vā—ayaṁ so bhagavā arahaṁ sammāsambuddho bhaggesu viharati susumāragire bhesakaḷāvane migadāye—taṁ bhagavantaṁ upasaṅkamitvā pucchatu.
root_text.an6.16:7.6: Tasmātiha tvaṁ, gahapati, mā sāpekkho kālamakāsi.
root_text.an6.16:8.1: Atha kho nakulapituno gahapatissa nakulamātarā gahapatāniyā iminā ovādena ovadiyamānassa so ābādho ṭhānaso paṭippassambhi.
root_text.an6.16:8.2: Vuṭṭhahi ca nakulapitā gahapati tamhā ābādhā;
root_text.an6.16:8.3: tathā pahīno ca pana nakulapituno gahapatissa so ābādho ahosi.
root_text.an6.16:8.4: Atha kho nakulapitā gahapati gilānā vuṭṭhito aciravuṭṭhito gelaññā daṇḍamolubbha yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinnaṁ kho nakulapitaraṁ gahapatiṁ bhagavā etadavoca:
root_text.an6.16:9.2: Yassa te nakulamātā gahapatānī anukampikā atthakāmā ovādikā anusāsikā.
root_text.an6.16:9.3: Yāvatā kho, gahapati, mama sāvikā gihī odātavasanā sīlesu paripūrakāriniyo, nakulamātā gahapatānī tāsaṁ aññatarā.
root_text.an6.16:9.4: Yāvatā kho, gahapati, mama sāvikā gihī odātavasanā lābhiniyo ajjhattaṁ cetosamathassa, nakulamātā gahapatānī tāsaṁ aññatarā.
root_text.an6.16:9.5: Yāvatā kho, gahapati, mama sāvikā gihī odātavasanā imasmiṁ dhammavinaye ogādhappattā patigādhappattā assāsappattā tiṇṇavicikicchā vigatakathaṅkathā vesārajjappattā aparappaccayā satthusāsane viharanti, nakulamātā gahapatānī tāsaṁ aññatarā.
root_text.an6.16:9.6: Lābhā te, gahapati, suladdhaṁ te, gahapati.
root_text.an6.16:9.7: Yassa te nakulamātā gahapatānī anukampikā atthakāmā ovādikā anusāsikā”ti.

translation_text.an6.16:1.1: At one time the Buddha was staying in the land of the Bhaggas at Crocodile’s Bellow, in the deer park at Bhesakaḷā’s Wood.
translation_text.an6.16:1.2: Now at that time the householder Nakula’s father was sick, suffering, gravely ill.
translation_text.an6.16:1.3: Then the housewife Nakula’s mother said to him:
translation_text.an6.16:2.3: and it’s criticized by the Buddha.
translation_text.an6.16:2.5: ‘When I’ve gone, the housewife Nakula’s mother won’t be able to provide for the children and keep up the household carpets.’
translation_text.an6.16:2.6: But you should not see it like this.
translation_text.an6.16:2.7: I’m skilled at spinning cotton and carding wool.
translation_text.an6.16:2.8: I’m able to provide for the children and keep up the household carpets.
translation_text.an6.16:2.9: So householder, don’t pass away with concerns …
translation_text.an6.16:3.2: ‘When I’ve gone, the housewife Nakula’s mother will take another husband.’
translation_text.an6.16:3.3: But you should not see it like this.
translation_text.an6.16:3.4: Both you and I know that we have remained chaste while at home for the past sixteen years.
translation_text.an6.16:3.5: So householder, don’t pass away with concerns …
translation_text.an6.16:4.2: ‘When I’ve gone, the housewife Nakula’s mother won’t want to see the Buddha and his Saṅgha of mendicants.’
translation_text.an6.16:4.3: But you should not see it like this.
translation_text.an6.16:4.4: When you’ve gone, I’ll want to see the Buddha and his mendicant Saṅgha even more.
translation_text.an6.16:4.5: So householder, don’t pass away with concerns …
translation_text.an6.16:5.2: ‘The housewife Nakula’s mother won’t fulfill ethics.’
translation_text.an6.16:5.3: But you should not see it like this.
translation_text.an6.16:5.4: I am one of those white-robed disciples of the Buddha who fulfills their ethics.
translation_text.an6.16:5.5: For whoever doubts this, that Blessed One, the perfected one, the fully awakened Buddha is staying in the land of the Bhaggas at Crocodile’s Bellow, in the deer park at Bhesakaḷā’s Wood. Let them approach him and ask.
translation_text.an6.16:5.6: So householder, don’t pass away with concerns …
translation_text.an6.16:6.2: ‘The housewife Nakula’s mother doesn’t have internal serenity of heart.’
translation_text.an6.16:6.3: But you should not see it like this.
translation_text.an6.16:6.4: I am one of those white-robed disciples of the Buddha who has internal serenity of heart.
translation_text.an6.16:6.5: For whoever doubts this, that Blessed One is staying in the land of the Bhaggas at Crocodile’s Bellow, in the deer park at Bhesakaḷā’s Wood. Let them approach him and ask.
translation_text.an6.16:6.6: So householder, don’t pass away with concerns …
translation_text.an6.16:7.2: ‘The housewife Nakula’s mother has not found a footing, a firm footing, and solace in this teaching and training. She has not gone beyond doubt, got rid of indecision, and gained assurance. And she’s not independent of others in the Teacher’s instructions.’
translation_text.an6.16:7.3: But you should not see it like this.
translation_text.an6.16:7.4: I am one of those white-robed disciples of the Buddha who has found a footing, a firm footing, and solace in this teaching and training. I have gone beyond doubt, got rid of indecision, and gained assurance. And I am independent of others in the Teacher’s instructions.
translation_text.an6.16:7.5: For whoever doubts this, that Blessed One is staying in the land of the Bhaggas at Crocodile’s Bellow, in the deer park at Bhesakaḷā’s Wood. Let them approach him and ask.
translation_text.an6.16:7.6: So householder, don’t pass away with concerns.
translation_text.an6.16:7.8: and it’s criticized by the Buddha.”
translation_text.an6.16:8.1: And then, as Nakula’s mother was giving this advice to Nakula’s father, his illness died down on the spot.
translation_text.an6.16:8.2: And that’s how Nakula’s father recovered from that illness.
translation_text.an6.16:8.4: Soon after recovering, leaning on a staff he went to the Buddha, bowed, and sat down to one side. The Buddha said to him:
translation_text.an6.16:9.2: to have the housewife Nakula’s mother advise and instruct you out of kindness and sympathy.
translation_text.an6.16:9.3: She is one of those white-robed disciples of the Buddha who fulfills their ethics.
translation_text.an6.16:9.4: She is one of those white-robed disciples of the Buddha who has internal serenity of heart.
translation_text.an6.16:9.5: She is one of those white-robed disciples of the Buddha who has found a footing, a firm footing, and solace in this teaching and training. She has gone beyond doubt, got rid of indecision, and gained assurance. And she is independent of others in the Teacher’s instructions.
translation_text.an6.16:9.6: You’re fortunate, householder, so very fortunate,
translation_text.an6.16:9.7: to have the housewife Nakula’s mother advise and instruct you out of kindness and sympathy.”

variant_text.an6.16:2.5: sakkhissati → na sakkhissati (sya-all, km); sakkoti (pts1ed, mr) | santharitun’ti → saṇṭharitunti (sya-all); santharitunti (pts1ed); sandharitunti (mr)
variant_text.an6.16:3.4: yaṁ no → yadā no (bj); yadā te (si); yathā (sya-all); yathā no (pts1ed) | samāciṇṇaṁ → samādinnaṁ (bj)
variant_text.an6.16:6.2: nakulamātā gahapatānī lābhinī → nakulamātā gahapatānī na lābhinī (pts1ed)
